title: Under Her Dark Veil
content:
Under her dark veil she wrung her hands.
"Why are you so pale today?"
"Because I made him drink of stinging grief
Until he got drunk on it.
How can I forget? He staggered out,
His mouth twisted in agony.
I ran down not touching the bannister

And caught up with him at the gate.
I cried: 'A joke!
That's all it was. If you leave, I'll die.'
He smiled calmly and grimly
And told me: 'Don't stand here in the wind.' "
meaning:
Anna Akhmatova's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Under Her Dark Veil"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
